evidence_label: case-001 clinician response label-capture gate, not medical advice

source_checks.source_role: Transfusion category, burden, response, and adverse-event context must be clinician-validated before advanced-option interpretation.
Iron, liver, cardiac, chelation, and organ-risk context must remain separate from treatment-lane enthusiasm.
Genotype, phenotype, and diagnostic confirmation remain upstream of case-specific treatment relevance.
Adult thalassemia regulatory context; not proof of individual fit, local access, affordability, or safety fit.
Approval and REMS/safety context for clinician-only review boundaries.
May 22, 2026 company announcement of European Commission adult thalassemia approval; local access still requires owner confirmation.
May 23 API check: pediatric TDT mitapivat remains not yet recruiting with no posted results.
May 23 API check: pediatric NTDT mitapivat remains not yet recruiting with no posted results.
May 23 API check: adult TDT mitapivat remains active-not-recruiting with posted results; benchmark context only until clinician review.

capture_flow.private_action: Save the original clinician reply only in the private workspace.
Mark which domain the clinician answered: diagnosis, transfusion dependence, burden, immune/blood-bank, iron/organ, referral, mitapivat, or owner routing.
Map the answer to confirmed, corrected, not confirmable, not applicable, or under specialist review.
Capture missing record categories and owner categories without naming people, hospitals, phone numbers, or exact dates.
If mitapivat is mentioned, classify it as adult-label context, adult-trial benchmark, pediatric-watchlist context, local-access owner question, safety-owner question, or not clinically relevant now.
Run public release review and update Git only with labels, missing categories, owner categories, and next gate.

decision_rule: A clinician answer changes the private work queue first. The public repository receives labels only. Adult mitapivat approvals and adult trial results update clinician-review context, but they do not close diagnosis, transfusion, safety, access, affordability, or individual-fit gates.
